ead.archdesc:
======= RMM04952Janet Fitchen papers4952Compiled by M.E. WarrenDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections
June 20042B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL:
socscThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:54:51 UTC.12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)2007-05-29
converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Janet Fitchen papers
Fitchen, Janet.
4952
3.4 cubic feet. 3.4 cubic feet.
1956-1995. Files, notes, correspondence, transcripts, manuscripts, maps, testimony. Subject files, research and field notes, correspondence, data and data analysis, transcripts, case files, manuscripts of books, maps, testimony, and other papers relating to Fitchen's work on rural poverty, mostly in New York State. Collection material in English 2828393
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
M.E. Warren
Date completed:
April 2004
EAD encoding:
Ann Hubert, June 2004
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Cultural anthropologist who concentrated on the rural United States. She wrote "Poverty in Rural America: A Case Study," an ethnographic study of poverty in upstate New York and "Endangered Spaces, Enduring Places: Change, Identity, and Survival in Rural America," which describes changes now transforming rural communities in the Northeast. In 1992-93, she was an Associate with the Western Rural Development Center in Corvallis, Oregon, where she conducted research on rural poverty in several other regions of the United States. She taught in the Anthropology Department at Ithaca College and the Department of Rural Sociology at Cornell University. She was a consultant and trainer for human service programs around the nation, including community action programs, social service agencies, and Cooperative Extension as well as Head Start and rural school districts, as well as serving on several national and state advisory boards related to rural poverty.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Subject files, research and field notes, correspondence, data and data analysis, transcripts, case files, manuscripts of books, maps, testimony, and other papers relating to Fitchen's work on rural poverty, mostly in New York State.
Cite As:
Janet Fitchen papers. #4952. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Access Restrictions:
Access restricted to the permission of the donor.
Rural poor -- New York (State) -- Study and teaching.
